summary.intent: Fix a stale-state bug in the Rust carousel component where clicking the autoplay trigger while `auto_play` is `None` dispatched `AutoPlayPause`, setting a `paused` flag that later suppressed the autoplay timer when autoplay was enabled.
summary.outcome: Bug fixed by guarding the trigger handler and both `AutoPlayPause`/`AutoPlayResume` transition arms with early-return when `auto_play` is `None`. 116 tests pass, clippy clean, xci-fast green, spec updated, and changes committed/pushed.

repo: The carousel state machine processes `AutoPlayPause` and `AutoPlayResume` messages even when `auto_play` is `None`, which can corrupt the `paused` flag and suppress timer startup — all three sites (trigger handler, Pause arm, Resume arm) must guard on `auto_play.is_some()`.
The component spec lives at `spec/components/layout/carousel.md` and must be kept in sync with implementation logic changes.
`cargo xci-fast` is the fast CI smoke-test command used before committing.

code.finding: AutoPlayPause and AutoPlayResume arms, plus the autoplay trigger handler, were missing `auto_play.is_some()` guards; idiomatic form uses early-return with `?` (clippy-enforced) rather than explicit `is_none()` checks.
Spec sections for AutoPlayPause, AutoPlayResume, and the trigger handler were updated to document the no-op behavior when `auto_play` is `None`.

workflow: Codex review passes surface specific message-dispatch bugs; pass-4 was the final review gate before commit.
When fixing a state machine bug, previously written tests may assert the buggy behavior and require updating — treat stale test failures as a signal to audit test intent, not just fix the assertion.
Clippy enforces idiomatic early-return style (`?`) over explicit `is_none()` + `return` guards; address before committing to keep CI clean.

friction: Two existing tests asserted the old buggy pause/resume behavior and required rewriting after the guard was introduced.
Clippy emitted 2 errors requiring conversion of `if self.auto_play.is_none() { return; }` patterns to `?`-style early returns, adding an extra fix cycle after the initial implementation.
